seed_problem: Given L printed layers of regions with required color tolerance classes and an asymmetric purge-cost matrix between concrete colors, choose a concrete color per region and a per-layer print order to minimize total purge volume across the whole job, including the carryover transition between consecutive layers.
mutation: composite: goal is genuinely open-ended construction (color assignment + ordering, no known optimum for large instances) AND the input is generalized with a THIRD degree of freedom -- each region's color is not fixed but drawn from a 2-3 option tolerance class -- turning what looks like a per-layer asymmetric-TSP touring problem into a global discrete recoloring problem that reshapes which transitions are even possible.
theme: Rainbow 3D print that bleeds between colors
title: Purge Carryover Scheduling: Rainbow Print Tolerance Recoloring
innovation_hook: The real leverage is in the discrete recoloring degrees of freedom that reshape the transition graph itself, not in touring the graph better: a bounded number of hard-locked anchor regions force a small, unavoidable number of expensive dark-to-light purges, but every other region's tolerance class contains an option in both the light and dark tiers -- a solver that recolors flex regions into whatever tier the CURRENT print run is committed to (ignoring which color is listed first) collapses nearly every layer-boundary carryover into a cheap transition, while a solver that only tours a fixed per-layer color assignment repeatedly eats extra full purges it could have recolored away.
strategies: trivial: print each layer's regions in listed input order, using each region's first-listed tolerance color -- the checker's own do-nothing baseline

greedy (trap): fix every region's color to its tolerance class's first-listed entry, then build a per-layer nearest-neighbour tour over that fixed assignment -- the textbook greedy-TSP move, locally sensible layer by layer, but blind to the next layer's carryover color and to the recoloring freedom, so trap regions (wrong color listed first) and layer-boundary mismatches repeatedly trigger the expensive purge

strong: sweep layers maintaining one global committed tier; recolor every flex region into whatever tier is currently committed (scanning its whole tolerance class, not just the first entry), place the committed-tier block before any hard-locked opposite-tier anchor within a layer, and only flip the committed tier when an anchor forces it -- collapses purge volume to close to the unavoidable anchor-pivot floor
invalid: emits a color id == C (out of range) for one region -- infeasible, must score 0
